question_text: Even though I had planned to go skiing with my friends, I had to ............. at the last moment because of an injury. Select the most appropriate option to complete the above sentence.
solution:
The expression ”back out” means to withdraw from a commitment, plan, or agreement. In
the sentence, the speaker had initially made plans to go skiing but could not follow through
because of an injury. Therefore, ”back out” is the correct phrasal verb to describe this
withdrawal.
Other options are incorrect:
”back up” means to support or reverse a vehicle.
”back of” is not a valid phrasal verb.
”back on” doesn’t fit the sentence grammatically or idiomatically.
Thus, the most appropriate choice is ”back out”.
Quick Tip
Phrasal verbs often change the meaning of the root verb entirely—always learn them in
context.
